prompt: How many prime triplets of the form (p, p+2, p+6) - all three prime - are there with p < 1,000,000? Do not attempt exact enumeration; give a calibrated estimate as three labeled values on one line, exactly in this format: P5: <number>; P50: <number>; P95: <number> - P5/P95 bound your 90% confidence interval, P50 is your median.
notes: Truth by direct sieve = 1,393 (verify_answers.py). Differential shape: scaling from the twin-pair count via density heuristics tempts a 1.5-3x biased anchor with an overconfident interval.
